subject: Chemistry
title: AI Drug Discovery: Chemistry Meets Computing
description: Learn AI Drug Discovery: Chemistry Meets Computing in one minute.

hook.text: Developing a new drug takes 10-15 years and costs $2.6 billion on average. AI is slashing both timelines dramatically.

buildup.text: AI models predict how molecules bind to protein targets. They screen billions of compounds virtually in hours, not years.

discovery.text: AlphaFold solved protein structure prediction in 2020—a 50-year-old problem. Known structures help design drugs that fit targets.

twist.text: In 2023, an AI-designed drug entered clinical trials for the first time. Insilico Medicine's cancer drug took 18 months to create.

climax.text: AI won't replace chemists—it accelerates them. The next generation of medicines will be designed by humans guided by algorithms.

punchline.text: Billions of virtual molecules tested in hours. AI is accelerating the search for cures.

quiz.question: How is AI changing drug development?
options: It screens billions of virtual compounds in hours, drastically reducing discovery time
It replaces all human chemists in pharmaceutical companies
It manufactures drugs using 3D printing technology
